title: New England travel tips for the last days of winter
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The South Boston St. Patrick's Day Parade on March 15 will reverse its route, expecting up to 1 million visitors.
The 43rd Annual Maine Maple Sunday weekend, featuring tours and tastings, is scheduled for March 21-22 statewide.
Halfinger Farms in Higganum, Connecticut, reopens April 1 with over 150,000 daffodils available for picking.

title: Fundraiser launched for family of Southborough crash victim, beloved local bartender
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A GoFundMe campaign has been set up for Crystal Johnson-Clementi, who died after a car accident in Southborough.
Johnson-Clementi, a bartender at Horseshoe Pub in Hudson, leaves behind her husband and son among other family members.
The fundraiser, started by her niece, has collected over $46,000, nearing its goal to support her family.

title: Jayson Tatum: Active for the game, never questionable in the tunnel
author: LaDonna LaGuerre
bullets: Jayson Tatum will return to play for the Boston Celtics at TD Garden after nearly 10 months out.
During his absence, Celtics teammates Neemias Queta and Jordan Walsh maintained the team's fashion excitement.
Tatum is renowned for pre-game fashion, with speculation about his outfit for the nationally televised return game.

title: How Susan Hurley’s legacy inspired her son to run the Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Max McGillivray is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in memory of his mother, Susan Hurley.
Susan Hurley created CharityTeams, raising $42 million for charity runners and nonprofits over nearly two decades.
McGillivray supports Justice Health Initiative, continuing his mother's work securing race bibs for healthcare fundraising.

title: Back Bay’s ‘ugly duckling’ is getting a makeover
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Developers are converting an 11-unit building at 18 Commonwealth Ave. into a luxury single-family home in Back Bay.
The renovation includes restoring the facade to match the neighborhood and adding modern amenities like a rooftop terrace.
Purchased in 2024 for $7.85 million, the transformed property will be listed for sale at $20-$24 million in May.

title: Country star Bailey Zimmerman surprises Boston bar crowd, picks up everyone’s tab
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Country musician Bailey Zimmerman visited J.J. Foley's Cafe in Boston and covered the entire bar's tab.
Zimmerman's unexpected visit followed a sold-out performance at TD Garden during his "Different Night Same Rodeo Tour."
J.J. Foley's Cafe is preparing for its 117th birthday and the start of Boston's St. Patrick's celebrations.

title: Haitian man living in Boston dies in ICE custody, heightening concerns about detention centers
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Emmanuel Damas, a 56-year-old Haitian asylum-seeker, died in ICE custody in Arizona after medical neglect of a toothache led to sepsis.
Family alleges ICE provided inadequate medical care, raising concerns about treatment in detention centers. Lawmakers sent a complaint to officials.
CoreCivic-run facility criticized for medical neglect. Sens. Markey and Warren, and Rep. Pressley demand accountability for Damas's preventable death.

title: Celtics officially list Jayson Tatum as ‘Available’ for game vs. Mavericks
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jayson Tatum is available for the Celtics' game against the Mavericks after recovering from a 2025 Achilles tendon rupture.
Boston currently holds a 41-21 record, exceeding preseason expectations, despite Tatum's previous absence due to injury.
The Celtics face lineup decisions as Tatum returns, including managing his playing time and adjusting the roster accordingly.

title: Patriots reportedly agree to two-year deal with backup QB Tommy DeVito
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Patriots sign 27-year-old QB Tommy DeVito to a two-year, $7.4 million deal with $2 million guaranteed.
DeVito joined the Patriots in 2025 after being claimed off waivers from the New York Giants.
DeVito will back up starter Drake Maye alongside veteran Josh Dobbs for the Patriots.

title: South Shore mother and son stuck in Qatar amid war in Iran
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: South Shore residents Stacey Schuhwerk and her son are stranded in Qatar due to the U.S.-Israeli attacks on Iran.
U.S. Rep. Stephen Lynch is assisting constituents stuck in the Middle East, including coordinating evacuation efforts.
Lynch criticizes Trump administration's handling of the crisis, citing missed opportunities for early evacuation of Americans.
